submit_spec.intent: Targeted short-tau Ramsey det-shift diagnostic on accepted r03: change det from 1.0 to 1.5 MHz to test whether the terminal ~1.19 MHz component tracks programmed det or is a baseline/artifact.

metadata.no_auto_align_reason: Accepted r03 has recent successful TrackCenter and terminal short-tau Ramsey final counts above the 20 kcps execute gate; use explicit tracked position and avoid hidden target reselection.

analysis_plan.notes: Compare whether the empirical component shifts by +0.5 MHz relative to the previous det=1.0 MHz short-tau run before any T2star/13C claim.

metadata.frequency_basis: fine weak-pi pODMR terminal raw/readout review selected grid-supported mw_freq=3.8759 GHz; det-shift diagnostic tests Ramsey carrier tracking, not resonance recentering.
metadata.det_choice_reason: Change det from 1.0 to 1.5 MHz. A physical Ramsey carrier underlying the prior ~1.192 MHz component should move to ~1.692 MHz; an artifact should not simply track the programmed det.
metadata.tau_window_reason: Reuse 48 ns..1.968 us / 41 points to isolate det change and avoid tau=0 artifact while resolving ~1.5-2.1 MHz targets below the 10.4 MHz Nyquist limit.
metadata.shot_reason: Reuse 12 x 90000 = 1.08e6 shots per tau point because the terminal det=1.0 component was just above per-point noise and this shot budget gave analyzable no-drift data.
metadata.even_average_reason: Use 12 averages under snake/even-average guidance; terminal short-tau drift diagnostic flagged no averages.

metadata.no_auto_align_reason: Accepted r03 has recent successful TrackCenter and terminal short-tau Ramsey final counts above the 20 kcps execute gate; use explicit tracked position and avoid hidden target reselection.

analysis_plan.notes: Compare whether the empirical component shifts by +0.5 MHz relative to the previous det=1.0 MHz short-tau run before any T2star/13C claim.

metadata.det_choice_reason: Change det from 1.0 to 1.5 MHz. A physical Ramsey carrier underlying the prior ~1.192 MHz component should move to ~1.692 MHz; an artifact should not simply track the programmed det.
metadata.tau_window_reason: Reuse 48 ns..1.968 us / 41 points to isolate det change and avoid tau=0 artifact while resolving ~1.5-2.1 MHz targets below the 10.4 MHz Nyquist limit.
metadata.shot_reason: Reuse 12 x 90000 = 1.08e6 shots per tau point because the terminal det=1.0 component was just above per-point noise and this shot budget gave analyzable no-drift data.
metadata.even_average_reason: Use 12 averages under snake/even-average guidance; terminal short-tau drift diagnostic flagged no averages.
metadata.intent: Targeted short-tau Ramsey det-shift diagnostic on accepted r03: change det from 1.0 to 1.5 MHz to test whether the terminal ~1.19 MHz component tracks programmed det or is a baseline/artifact.

Execute remains separately gated by bootstrap and local lab validation.

recipe.name: xml_generic_v1
notes: Generic XML route backed by a sequence manifest.
This is the only mainline recipe in the bridge; ODMR, Rabi, Ramsey, and future sequences should all enter through xml_generic_v1.
validate_job.m resolves sequence_manifest_id and enforces manifest bounds before any execute path is reached; job sample_id owns target identity.
Execute remains subject to the same explicit lab-only opt-in gate used by earlier curated routes.
Archived curated wrappers and recipe definitions are preserved under claw/legacy for historical reference only.

guidance.shot_preservation_guidance: When the predicted per-average tracking window is too long, first reduce repetitions per average and compensate by increasing averages so repetitions * averages remains as close as possible to the original total shots. If that is still insufficient, split the acquisition into shorter jobs. Reduce total shots only as a last resort.

split the acquisition into shorter jobs before reducing total shots
reduce total shots only as a last resort
